english_name: The First Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

verses.latin_vulgate: Fidelis sermo: si quis episcopatum desiderat, bonum opus desiderat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος εἴ τις ἐπισκοπῆς ὀρέγεται καλοῦ ἔργου ἐπιθυμεῖ
verses.king_james: This *is* a true saying, If a man desire the office of a bishop, he desireth a good work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oportet ergo episcopum irreprehensibilem esse, unius uxoris virum, sobrium, prudentem, ornatum, pudicum, hospitalem, doctorem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεῖ οὖν τὸν ἐπίσκοπον ἀνεπίληπτον εἶναι μιᾶς γυναικὸς ἄνδρα νηφάλεον σώφρονα κόσμιον φιλόξενον διδακτικόν
verses.king_james: A bishop then must be blameless, the husband of one wife, vigilant, sober, of good behaviour, given to hospitality, apt to teach;

verses.latin_vulgate: non vinolentum, non percussorem, sed modestum: non litigiosum, non cupidum, sed
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ πάροινον μὴ πλήκτην μὴ αἰσχροκερδῆ ἀλλ᾽ ἐπιεικῆ ἄμαχον ἀφιλάργυρον
verses.king_james: Not given to wine, no striker, not greedy of filthy lucre; but patient, not a brawler, not covetous;

verses.latin_vulgate: suæ domui bene præpositum: filios habentem subditos cum omni castitate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ ἰδίου οἴκου καλῶς προϊστάμενον τέκνα ἔχοντα ἐν ὑποταγῇ μετὰ πάσης σεμνότητος
verses.king_james: One that ruleth well his own house, having his children in subjection with all gravity;

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis autem domui suæ præesse nescit, quomodo ecclesiæ Dei diligentiam habebit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δέ τις τοῦ ἰδίου οἴκου προστῆναι οὐκ οἶδεν πῶς ἐκκλησίας θεοῦ ἐπιμελήσεται
verses.king_james: (For if a man know not how to rule his own house, how shall he take care of the church of God?)

verses.latin_vulgate: Non neophytum: ne in superbiam elatus, in judicium incidat diaboli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ νεόφυτον ἵνα μὴ τυφωθεὶς εἰς κρίμα ἐμπέσῃ τοῦ διαβόλου
verses.king_james: Not a novice, lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oportet autem illum et testimonium habere bonum ab iis qui foris sunt, ut non in opprobrium incidat, et in laqueum diaboli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεῖ δὲ αὐτὸν καὶ μαρτυρίαν καλὴν ἔχειν ἀπὸ τῶν ἔξωθεν ἵνα μὴ εἰς ὀνειδισμὸν ἐμπέσῃ καὶ παγίδα τοῦ διαβόλου
verses.king_james: Moreover he must have a good report of them which are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Diaconos similiter pudicos, non bilingues, non multo vino deditos, non turpe lucrum sectantes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διακόνους ὡσαύτως σεμνούς μὴ διλόγους μὴ οἴνῳ πολλῷ προσέχοντας μὴ αἰσχροκερδεῖς
verses.king_james: Likewise *must* the deacons *be* grave, not doubletongued, not given to much wine, not greedy of filthy lucre;

verses.latin_vulgate: habentes mysterium fidei in conscientia pura.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχοντας τὸ μυστήριον τῆς πίστεως ἐν καθαρᾷ συνειδήσει
verses.king_james: Holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hi autem probentur primum: et sic ministrent, nullum crimen habentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: kai outoi de dokimazesqwsan prwton eita diakoneitwsan anegklhtoi ontej
verses.king_james: And let these also first be proved; then let them use the office of a deacon, being *found* blameless.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulieres similiter pudicas, non detrahentes, sobrias, fideles in omnibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γυναῖκας ὡσαύτως σεμνάς μὴ διαβόλους νηφαλέους πιστὰς ἐν πᾶσιν
verses.king_james: Even so *must their* wives *be* grave, not slanderers, sober, faithful in all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Diaconi sint unius uxoris viri, qui filiis suis bene præsint, et suis domibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διάκονοι ἔστωσαν μιᾶς γυναικὸς ἄνδρες τέκνων καλῶς προϊστάμενοι καὶ τῶν ἰδίων οἴκων
verses.king_james: Let the deacons be the husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui enim bene ministraverint, gradum bonum sibi acquirent, et multam fiduciam in fide, quæ est in Christo Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ γὰρ καλῶς διακονήσαντες βαθμὸν ἑαυτοῖς καλὸν περιποιοῦνται καὶ πολλὴν παῤῥησίαν ἐν πίστει τῇ ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: For they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc tibi scribo, sperans me ad te venire cito:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτά σοι γράφω ἐλπίζων ἐλθεῖν πρὸς σὲ τάχιον
verses.king_james: These things write I unto thee, hoping to come unto thee shortly:

verses.latin_vulgate: si autem tardavero, ut scias quomodo oporteat te in domo Dei conversari, quæ est ecclesia Dei vivi, columna et firmamentum veritatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν δὲ βραδύνω ἵνα εἰδῇς πῶς δεῖ ἐν οἴκῳ θεοῦ ἀναστρέφεσθαι ἥτις ἐστὶν ἐκκλησία θεοῦ ζῶντος στῦλος καὶ ἑδραίωμα τῆς ἀληθείας
verses.king_james: But if I tarry long, that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et manifeste magnum est pietatis sacramentum, quod manifestatum est in carne, justificatum est in spiritu, apparuit angelis, prædicatum est gentibus, creditum est in mundo, assumptum est in gloria.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁμολογουμένως μέγα ἐστὶν τὸ τῆς εὐσεβείας μυστήριον Θεὸς ἐφανερώθη ἐν σαρκί ἐδικαιώθη ἐν πνεύματι ὤφθη ἀγγέλοις ἐκηρύχθη ἐν ἔθνεσιν ἐπιστεύθη ἐν κόσμῳ ἀνελήφθη ἐν δόξῃ
verses.king_james: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: God was manifest in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen of angels, preached unto the Gentiles, believed on in the world, received up into glory.
